page.content: In examining the contrasting frameworks of health beliefs and practices, the data presented in the table elucidates the significant disparities between traditional health models and Western health paradigms. The column titled "Traditional model-beliefs and practice" highlights the cultural and historical underpinnings that shape health behaviors within various communities, often emphasizing holistic approaches and community-driven interventions. Conversely, the "Western health model: effects of reported behaviour on health" column provides insight into how behaviors, as reported by individuals within these frameworks, influence health outcomes.

page.html:
Traditional model-beliefs and practiceWestern health model: effects of reported behaviour on health, and basis for this judgement
ThemesBeliefsWhat is reported to be actually happeningEffects of actual behaviour on healthExplanation of these effects
Dietary precautionsEating more foodAll consumed more food than usual.Beneficial [23]Provides adequate nutrients for lactation.
Eating hot food (protein rich)All consumed these foods.Beneficial[21,22,23]Provides adequate nutrients for lactation. Provides nutrients that promote healing of tears and incisions.
Avoiding cold food (fruit and vegetables)All ate vegetables, but less amount and fewer kinds.No obvious health effectsIf very restricted, could cause constipation. However, women did not report constipation as a problem.
HygieneNo bathing or washing hairRural: Most bathed. Urban: Most used dry or damp towel to clean skin. Half washed hair.No obvious health effects
Vulval and perineal hygiene is importantAll did daily vulval and perineal hygiene.Beneficial [24,25]Promotes healing of tears and incisions, prevents infections.
No brushing teeth.Most did not practice pre-birth dental hygiene habits.Harmful [31,32]Allows a build up of plaque and bacteria that can cause teeth decay.
Behavioural precautionsStaying inside the homeAll stayed in their homes.No obvious health effects
Avoiding houseworkMost did no housework.BeneficialAllows time to recover from childbirth and focus on caring for the baby.
Resting in bed.Women walked around room or home. Few did other exercises.No obvious health effectsWith complete bed rest, deep vein thrombosis is a risk but no women reported this.
Abstaining from sexAll followed this restriction.No obvious health effectsAllows time for the reproductive tract tissues to recover and heal.
Limiting visitorsMost families had fewer visitors.No obvious health effects
Infant feedingBreastfeeding is bestAll breastfed, but only two exclusively. Most gave supplements in first 3 days and continued for whole period.Harmful [37-40]Interferes with milk production and breastfeeding technique, possible source of infection.
Giving honeysuckle for skin rashRural: infrequently. Urban: common.Unknown

cells.cell_text: Western health model: effects of reported behaviour on health, and basis for this judgement
What is reported to be actually happening
Effects of actual behaviour on health
All consumed more food than usual.
Provides adequate nutrients for lactation. Provides nutrients that promote healing of tears and incisions.
All ate vegetables, but less amount and fewer kinds.
If very restricted, could cause constipation. However, women did not report constipation as a problem.
Rural: Most bathed. Urban: Most used dry or damp towel to clean skin. Half washed hair.
Vulval and perineal hygiene is important
All did daily vulval and perineal hygiene.
Promotes healing of tears and incisions, prevents infections.
Most did not practice pre-birth dental hygiene habits.
Allows a build up of plaque and bacteria that can cause teeth decay.
Allows time to recover from childbirth and focus on caring for the baby.
Women walked around room or home. Few did other exercises.
With complete bed rest, deep vein thrombosis is a risk but no women reported this.
Allows time for the reproductive tract tissues to recover and heal.
All breastfed, but only two exclusively. Most gave supplements in first 3 days and continued for whole period.
Interferes with milk production and breastfeeding technique, possible source of infection.

page.content: This juxtaposition serves to illustrate not only the efficacy of different health practices but also the broader implications of these behaviors on public health metrics. Furthermore, the "basis for this judgement" column offers a critical analysis of the rationale behind these assessments, drawing on empirical evidence and theoretical perspectives. By synthesizing these elements, the table presents a comprehensive overview of how differing beliefs and reported behaviors can lead to varied health outcomes, thereby underscoring the importance of contextually relevant health interventions that acknowledge both traditional and contemporary health models. This analysis is essential for informing policy decisions and tailoring health initiatives to meet the diverse needs of populations.
